description: Progression and Play - branching evolution driven by real HealthKit behaviour, a full 1,022-entry Dex with subtle evolution hints, visible sickness, battles with per-Digimon projectiles and signature moves, and six interactive training minigames. Source PRD: tasks/prd-progression-and-play.md

userStories.title: Spike - which HealthKit types are actually readable on watchOS 26.4
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to know which health identifiers exist, are readable on the watch, and return data, before designing evolution criteria around ones that do not.
acceptanceCriteria: For each candidate identifier, record three facts: compiles on watchOS 26.4, appears in the authorization sheet, and returns samples or noData (not an error) in the Simulator

Quantity candidates probed: .stepCount, .distanceWalkingRunning, .flightsClimbed, .appleExerciseTime, .appleStandTime, .activeEnergyBurned, .basalEnergyBurned, .vo2Max, .restingHeartRate, .heartRateVariabilitySDNN, .respiratoryRate, .oxygenSaturation, .distanceSwimming, .distanceCycling, .dietaryWater, .timeInDaylight, .physicalEffort, .environmentalAudioExposure
Category candidates probed: .handwashingEvent, .mindfulSession, .appleStandHour, .toothbrushingEvent, .sleepAnalysis, .highHeartRateEvent, .lowCardioFitnessEvent, .appleWalkingSteadinessEvent
HKWorkoutType.workoutType() probed - whether workout counts by activity type are readable
docs/health-metrics.md exists with one row per identifier and a verdict of usable / unavailable on watchOS / compiles but never has data
The doc explicitly records which identifiers need a NEW authorization prompt versus which are covered by the four already granted (steps, activeEnergy, sleep, exercise)
Any probe code is under #if DEBUG and ships no user-facing feature
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
userStories.notes: DONE. All 27 identifiers + HKWorkoutType compile, appear in the auth sheet, and are VALID readable types on watchOS 26.4 - zero returned errorInvalidArgument(3); the 23 un-granted ones returned errorAuthorizationNotDetermined(5), a grant failure not a type failure. The 4 already-granted (steps, activeEnergy, exercise, sleep) read cleanly as ok(samples=0). docs/health-metrics.md has a row + verdict for every identifier. CAVEAT US-061 MUST HEED: nothing is certified as HAVING data - the Simulator health DB is empty (even granted types gave samples=0) and cannot be granted (simctl privacy has no 'health' service), so the 'compiles but never has data' verdict could be assigned to nothing AND ruled out for nothing. Doc names 7 at-risk iPhone-sourced types to use only as gracefully-degrading bonus branches.

userStories.title: Add EvolutionCondition to the edge schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need evolution conditions to be data in evolutions.json so criteria can be authored without editing Swift.
acceptanceCriteria: New EvolutionCondition struct with fields: metric (string enum), window (stage | day | lifetime), comparison (atLeast | atMost), value (Double), hint (String)

The metric enum spans TWO families, so conditions are the single gating vocabulary: health.* (HealthKit-backed, only identifiers US-055 marked usable) and care.* (game counters that nothing today can express: trainingSessions, overfeeds, sleepDisturbances, battleCount, battleWinRatio). careMistakes is deliberately NOT a condition metric - the existing maxCareMistakes edge field already gates on it, and two ways to say one thing invites a later iteration to delete one

A BAND is expressed as two conditions on one edge (atLeast X + atMost Y), reproducing the Digital Monster Color pattern where training 8-31 earns the good branch but 0-7 AND 32+ both fall to the junk one - overtraining is punished as much as undertraining
battleWinRatio is a 0.0-1.0 fraction, so DMC's '15+ battles at 80%+ wins' is expressible as care.battleCount atLeast 15 + care.battleWinRatio atLeast 0.8
minBattleWins stays and keeps working; care.battleWinRatio ADDS ratio gating, which minBattleWins cannot express. An edge may use either or both
EvolutionEdge gains conditions: [EvolutionCondition], hand-decoded to default [] when the key is absent - matching how isDefault and dexOnly already default in EvolutionGraph.swift
Every existing edge in Resources/evolutions.json decodes unchanged with conditions == []
EvolutionGraphValidator rejects an unknown metric, a negative value, an empty hint, and a battleWinRatio outside 0.0-1.0
docs/evolutions-schema.md gains a Condition section documenting both metric families, every window value, and the two-condition band idiom
Existing Tests/EvolutionGraphTests.swift and Tests/EvolutionGraphValidatorTests.swift still pass untouched
Add any new files to project.yml and re-run xcodegen generate - never hand-edit .xcodeproj
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: EvolutionCondition + ConditionMetric (27 health.* from US-055's usable list, 5 care.*, no careMistakes) in Sources/EvolutionCondition.swift; edge gains conditions hand-decoded to []. metric is a STRING with knownMetric accessor, not a typed enum, so an unknown metric is a VALIDATOR error not a launch trap. 4 new validator rules + schema doc Condition section. 668 tests green (was 647); evolutions.json and both existing Evolution*Tests.swift untouched.

userStories.title: Read an arbitrary health metric over a window
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one reader that totals any usable metric - quantity or category - over a date interval, so conditions are not limited to the four energy metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: New HealthMetricReader totals a quantity metric over an injected DateInterval
It counts a category metric's EVENTS rather than summing values, since HKCategoryValueNotApplicable has no meaningful magnitude
.appleStandHour counts only samples whose value is HKCategoryValueAppleStandHour.stood
Reads sit behind a protocol so tests inject fixture samples - the Simulator has no health data and a test against a live query proves nothing
A failing or unauthorized read returns .unavailable and never throws, matching TodayHealthReader.read
Tests cover: a quantity total, a category event count, a stand-hour filtered count, an empty window returning noData, and a failing fetch returning .unavailable
Add new files to project.yml and re-run xcodegen generate
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: HealthMetricReader + ReadableHealthMetric (failable init: care.* and health.sleep are not constructible, so an unreadable metric cannot compile into a wrong answer). Aggregations: sumQuantity / averageQuantity (discrete types) / countEvents / countStoodHours / sumDurationMinutes. Reads behind HealthMetricSampleFetching; failure -> .unavailable, empty window -> .noData. 16 new tests, suite 684/0 failures, BUILD SUCCEEDED.

userStories.title: Accumulate per-stage metric totals
userStories.description: As a developer, I need running totals per metric since the Digimon entered its current stage, so a window:stage condition has something to compare against.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState gains stage-scoped metric totals keyed by metric, and they persist across app launches
Crediting is idempotent - refreshing twice in one day does not double-count, following the existing EnergyLedger pattern
Stage totals reset on evolution, when stageEnteredDate moves
Lifetime totals accumulate separately and never reset
window:day resolves to the BEST single local day, not the current one, so one good Tuesday still counts on Friday
All tests drive an injectable clock and none waits real time
Tests cover: accumulation, double-refresh idempotency, reset on evolution, lifetime survival across a stage reset, and best-day selection
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: MetricLedger + MetricCreditor bank per-metric daily readings into GameState's stageMetricTotals / lifetimeMetricTotals / stageBestDayMetrics, on EnergyLedger's delta pattern. Only ADDITIVE metrics accumulate (averageQuantity ones are excluded by ConditionMetric.accumulatesOverTime). enterStage(at:) owns the stage reset. 18 new tests, 702/702 green.

userStories.title: Stage-scoped care counters for evolution gating
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the counters the Digital Monster Color branches on - training sessions, overfeeds, sleep disturbances - tracked per stage, since none of them exists in a usable form today.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState gains stage-scoped trainingSessions, overfeeds and sleepDisturbances counters, all persisted
trainingSessions increments once per session REGARDLESS of the minigame grade - DMC counts training whether it succeeded or not, and US-075's graded gain must not be the thing evolution reads
overfeeds counts cumulative refusals within the stage - distinct from the existing refusalCount, which resets daily and so cannot express a stage-long 3+ overfeed gate
sleepDisturbances counts waking the Digimon during its sleep window - a new counter; only wakeMistakeDay exists today, and it is a day marker rather than a count
All three reset on evolution, when stageEnteredDate moves, alongside the US-058 metric totals
care.battleWinRatio derives from the existing battleWins and battleLosses and is 0.0 when no battle has been fought - never a divide by zero
The existing refusalCount, wakeMistakeDay and care-mistake behaviour are unchanged; this story ADDS counters and rewires nothing
Tests drive an injectable clock and cover: each counter incrementing, reset on evolution, ratio with zero battles, and trainingSessions incrementing on a miss-graded session
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Stage-scoped stageTrainingSessions/stageOverfeeds/stageSleepDisturbances on GameState, persisted as optional Int storage (same migration shape as every other property there) and cleared by enterStage alongside the US-058 metric totals. Incremented at the existing event sites: TrainAction.train (on .trained only, via recordTrainingSession(), grade-blind), recordRefusal (outside the daily rollover), recordWakingEarly (before the once-a-day guard, so every disturbance counts). battleWinRatio is derived from battleWins/battleLosses and returns 0.0 at zero battles rather than NaN. refusalCount, wakeMistakeDay and the care-mistake rules are untouched and pinned by tests. 15 tests in Tests/CareCounterTests.swift; full suite 717 tests, 0 failures; build green.

userStories.title: Expand HealthKit authorization without breaking the existing grant
userStories.description: As a user who already granted the four original types, I want the new ones requested without losing what I already approved.
acceptanceCriteria: The read set includes every metric named by a condition in evolutions.json, DERIVED from the graph rather than hardcoded, so authoring a new condition cannot forget the grant
A user holding the original four grants is re-prompted only for the new types
A denied new type makes its conditions unsatisfiable but leaves every other edge working - it never blocks evolution entirely and never charges a care mistake
HealthAuthorizationView copy explains what the new types are for
The existing -healthDenied, -healthUnavailable and -healthAnswered launch flags still work
Existing Tests/HealthAuthorizationTests.swift still passes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: HealthReadSet unions the four energy metrics with every health.* metric named by a condition in the graph, DERIVED via EvolutionGraph.conditionHealthMetrics; HealthAuthorizing now takes the whole set so status and request both cover it in one call. 20 new tests, 737 total, 0 failures. CAVEATS: (a) 're-prompted only for the new types' is HealthKit's own documented behaviour for already-answered types - what is TESTED is the app-side mechanics (single request, wide status check, additionalObjectTypes), not a real prompt on hardware; (b) 'a denied type makes its conditions unsatisfiable' is US-060's own AC - what is verified here is the blast radius: a failed read is .unavailable and never a throw, and the care-mistake verdict is structurally unable to see a condition metric.

userStories.title: EvolutionEngine evaluates conditions
userStories.description: As a player, I want my real behaviour to decide which evolution branch I get.
acceptanceCriteria: EvolutionEngine.qualifies returns false unless EVERY condition on the edge passes
Existing gates (requiredEnergy, minEnergy, maxCareMistakes, minBattleWins) are unchanged and still evaluated
An edge with conditions == [] behaves exactly as it does today - every existing assertion in Tests/EvolutionTests.swift passes untouched
A metric whose reading is .unavailable FAILS its condition rather than passing it, so an unreadable metric never hands out a branch the player did not earn
The isDefault fallback still fires when nothing qualifies, so no Digimon is ever permanently stuck
A band (atLeast + atMost on the same metric) is evaluated as a closed interval, and a value outside it fails - so an overtrained Digimon falls to the junk branch exactly as it does on the real device
care.* conditions read the US-084 counters; health.* conditions read the US-058 totals
Tests cover: a condition met, a condition unmet, atMost inverted, multiple conditions where one fails, an unavailable metric, and the fallback firing when all conditioned edges fail
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: EvolutionEngine.qualifies now also requires every EvolutionCondition on the edge, answered by new ConditionEvaluator/ConditionContext (Sources/ConditionEvaluator.swift): health.* accumulating metrics read the US-058 stage/lifetime/best-day totals, standing measurements read a direct HealthReading, care.* reads the US-084 counters. Unknown or unavailable fails the condition in BOTH directions, so a denied metric never passes an atMost gate. Bands are closed intervals; the isDefault fallback still fires. 13 tests in Tests/ConditionEvaluationTests.swift; suite 752 tests / 0 failures, build green.

userStories.title: Author branching criteria and junk evolutions
userStories.description: As a player, I want each Digimon to have a real choice with a real consequence for neglect, so an Agumon that never walked becomes Numemon rather than Greymon.
acceptanceCriteria: Every non-terminal Child and Adult across the six shipped lines has two to three outgoing edges
Every such node's isDefault edge is a junk evolution reachable by inaction
Numemon (Adult/Numemon.png) and Scumon (Adult/Scumon.png) are verified present with ls before being referenced
At least one node uses the DMC band shape - a middle training range earns the good branch while both too-little and too-much fall to the junk one
At least one Perfect-or-later edge is gated on battle performance as a RATIO, e.g. care.battleCount atLeast 15 plus care.battleWinRatio atLeast 0.8, following the real device
At least EIGHT distinct condition metrics are used across the file, drawn from BOTH the health.* and care.* families
Every 'to' names a node that exists, and every spriteFile is verified with ls before use
No dexOnly node is named by any edge
EvolutionGraphValidator passes on the whole file
A test asserts no condition hint string contains a digit
Only health.* metrics US-055 marked usable are authored
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Authored across all six lines: 88 nodes (was 69). Every non-terminal Child and Adult now has 2-3 edges whose isDefault is a junk evolution reachable by inaction, and each line's default path is a complete Digitama-to-Ultimate JUNK path. Nine metrics from both families; Greymon carries the DMC training band (atLeast 8 / atMost 31); Etemon -> BanchoLeomon is gated on care.battleCount atLeast 15 plus care.battleWinRatio atLeast 0.8. Tests: Tests/EvolutionCriteriaTests.swift (17 assertions covering AC1-AC11), full suite 768 tests / 0 failures, build green. KNOWN GAP, NOT AN AC: MetricCreditor is still not called from MainScreenModel.refresh, so stageMetricTotals is always zero in the running app and every health.* atLeast gate fails live. Care.* gates work. Until that is wired, the app routes most Digimon down the junk branches. This needs its own story.

userStories.title: Bundle the full 1,022-entry roster
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the whole roster available at runtime so the Dex can show every Digimon.
acceptanceCriteria: Resources/roster.json is bundled and contains all 1,022 entries
New RosterEntry type with id, displayName, stage, spriteFile, variant, dexOnly - and deliberately NO line and NO evolutions
Entries that roster.generated.json emits with a null stage are resolved to a real stage before bundling; the loader rejects a null stage rather than defaulting one
A test verifies every spriteFile in roster.json exists on disk - by test, not by inspection
Resources/evolutions.json is UNMODIFIED by this story
scripts/ regenerates roster.json reproducibly and README.md documents the command
roster.json added to project.yml as a resource and xcodegen generate re-run
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Kept separate from evolutions.json on purpose: EvolutionNode.line is decode, not decodeIfPresent, so a missing line fails the WHOLE graph load, which fatalErrors at launch. Pouring ~950 line-less entries in would either break the load or force line optional and silently drop nodes from every tree.

userStories.title: Replace the Dex line list with a flat item grid
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see every Digimon in one grid so I can tell at a glance how much of the roster I have met.
acceptanceCriteria: The Dex root is a LazyVGrid over all 1,022 roster entries, not the line list
Owned entries show the idle sprite; unowned show '?', are visibly dimmed, and are .disabled
Only on-screen cells decode art - scrolling the grid must not decode 1,022 sprites
The header still reads discovered/total against the full roster
Tapping an owned cell opens DexDetailView; tapping an unowned one does nothing
Fits the 41mm screen without horizontal clipping
Scrolling stays responsive with no visible stutter on Series 11 (46mm)
Sprite rendering still uses .interpolation(.none)
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag and record the screenshot result in notes - simctl cannot synthesise a tap, so the screen must be pushable from the launch command
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Detail view lists possible evolutions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what my Digimon can become so I have something to aim at.
acceptanceCriteria: DexDetailView gains an 'Evolves into' section listing every outgoing edge's target
Each candidate shows its sprite and name if already discovered; a '?' with the name withheld if not
An entry with no edges, or absent from EvolutionGraph.bundled, shows 'No evolutions recorded.' rather than an empty section
One to three candidates render without scrolling on 41mm; more than three scrolls
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via the existing -dexDetailDemo flag and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Hint vocabulary for every condition metric
userStories.description: As a player, I want to be nudged toward what a branch needs without being handed a checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: Every HealthConditionMetric has default flavour text - e.g. steps 'Restless. It wants to see the horizon.', handwashing 'It flinches from grime.', mindful 'It listens for stillness.', stand hours 'It cannot bear sitting still.', daylight 'It turns toward the sun.'
An edge's authored hint overrides the metric default
A test asserts NO hint string contains a digit, so a hint can never leak its threshold
Hint resolution is a pure function, testable without a view
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Render hints with progress-based reveal
userStories.description: As a player, I want the hint to warm up as I get closer, so I can tell I am on the right track.
acceptanceCriteria: Three reveal levels chosen by progress toward the condition: far (< 50%) shows flavour text alone, close (>= 50%) shows flavour plus a warmer qualifier, met shows a checkmark
No exact number, threshold, or percentage is ever displayed
A candidate whose conditions are all met is visually distinguished from one that is not
The reveal-level function is pure and unit-tested at each boundary (just under 50%, exactly 50%, exactly met)
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag seeding a part-met condition, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Keep the evolution tree reachable from the flat Dex
userStories.description: As a user, I want the flat grid as my way in without losing the line tree, so the at-a-glance roster and the shape of a line are both available.
acceptanceCriteria: EvolutionTreeView is KEPT, not deleted - US-041/US-042 built it and it shows a line's branching shape, which the flat grid cannot
The flat grid from US-063 stays the Dex root, per the request that the line map was too complicated as an entry point
DexDetailView gains a way through to the owning line's tree for any Digimon that belongs to one
A Digimon in no line (the ~950 roster-only entries) shows no tree affordance rather than an empty tree
The -dexLineDemo launch flag still works
Tests/EvolutionTreeTests.swift and Tests/DexScreenTests.swift both still pass
Verify in the watchOS Simulator that the grid -> detail -> tree path works, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Deliberately a MERGE, not a retirement. An earlier draft offered deleting the tree; that discards working, tested UI to satisfy a navigation change. The flat grid answers 'what do I own', the tree answers 'what shape is this line' - both are worth keeping.

userStories.title: Animate the sick Digimon
userStories.description: As a user, I want a sick Digimon to look sick, so I notice without opening a menu.
acceptanceCriteria: A sick Digimon plays a distinct animation - the hurt loop (frames 9 <-> 10) at a SLOWER cadence than the battle hurt loop, so it reads as ailing rather than as being struck
Wandering movement is suppressed or slowed while sick
A healthy Digimon is visually unchanged
Sprite rendering still uses .interpolation(.none)
The animation choice is a pure function of healthStatus, unit-tested without a view
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag that forces the sick state, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Use a demo launch flag to reach the sick state. DO NOT uninstall the app to reset the save - progress.txt records that this wipes the HealthKit grant and strands the app on an untappable system sheet.

userStories.title: Pulsing medical-bandage icon
userStories.description: As a user, I want an unambiguous sick indicator, since a slow hurt loop alone is easy to miss.
acceptanceCriteria: A bandage.fill SF Symbol overlays the main screen while healthStatus == .sick
It pulses continuously via opacity or scale - not a one-shot animation
It is absent when healthy and absent when dead
It does not overlap the sprite's body or the action buttons on 41mm
The main screen still fits without scrolling - US-039 is not regressed
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via the same sick-state launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Move catalog
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-Digimon attack identity as data, so authoring a move is not a code change.
acceptanceCriteria: Resources/moves.json maps a Digimon id to projectileSymbol, tint, signatureName and signatureSymbol
Symbols are SF Symbols (flame.fill, bolt.fill, drop.fill, hand.raised.fill, snowflake, leaf.fill, moon.fill, sparkles, ...) - no new image assets
A test verifies every symbol name renders; an unknown symbol fails the test rather than shipping as a blank square
A Digimon absent from the file falls back by line, then by stage, so all 1,022 resolve to something and battle never renders an empty projectile
MoveCatalog lookup is pure and unit-tested including BOTH fallback tiers
moves.json added to project.yml as a resource and xcodegen generate re-run
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Face-to-face battle arena
userStories.description: As a user, I want the two Digimon facing each other, not both facing right.
acceptanceCriteria: Player on the left facing right; opponent on the right horizontally mirrored to face left
Mirroring does not soften the pixels - .interpolation(.none) survives the transform
Hit points and the opponent's name remain visible and the arena fits 41mm
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG battle demo launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Projectiles fly on each exchange
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the attack travel, so an exchange reads as a hit rather than two sprites twitching.
acceptanceCriteria: On each BattleTurn the attacker's projectile animates from attacker toward defender
It travels in the correct direction for whichever side is attacking
It is tinted with the attacker's MoveCatalog tint
Impact coincides with the defender entering its hurt loop - the existing frame assignment (attacker holds frame 11, defender plays 9 <-> 10) is preserved
Timing is driven by the existing injectable turnDuration, so tests still run a whole battle in milliseconds
BattleEngine is NOT touched - resolution stays pure and every existing assertion in Tests/BattleTests.swift and Tests/BattlePowerTests.swift holds
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via the battle demo flag, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Signature move on the finishing blow
userStories.description: As a user, I want the killing blow to be special and named, so a win feels like a win.
acceptanceCriteria: The turn where isKnockout is true renders the winner's signatureSymbol instead of the ordinary projectile
It is visibly larger than an ordinary projectile
The signatureName is shown as a banner during that turn
It fires whether the PLAYER or the OPPONENT lands the finish
The result screen still shows the existing win/loss frames and its haptic
Knockout-turn detection is pure and unit-tested against a seeded BattleReport
Verify in the watchOS Simulator and record the screenshot of a signature move in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Author moves for all 69 curated Digimon
userStories.description: As a player, I want each Digimon's attack to suit it, so owning a new one is worth looking forward to.
acceptanceCriteria: All 69 nodes in evolutions.json have an explicit moves.json entry
Attacks suit the Digimon - e.g. Agumon throws orange flame with a larger flame signature, Gabumon throws BLUE flame, a plant line throws leaves, an electric line throws bolts
No two Digimon in the same line share an identical projectileSymbol + tint pair
At least EIGHT distinct symbols are used across the file
Signature names are authored, not generated from a template
A test asserts full coverage of the 69 and the in-line uniqueness rule
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Minigame foundation and graded gain
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one shape every training minigame conforms to, so adding a seventh is adding a file.
acceptanceCriteria: A TrainingMinigame protocol (or enum) with a uniform TrainingResult: miss | good | great | perfect
TrainAction maps the result to a strengthStat gain of miss=0, good=1, great=2, perfect=3
Energy cost is charged ONCE on entering the game and is NOT refunded on miss
The session increments GameState.trainingSessions (US-084) exactly once regardless of grade, including on a miss - evolution branches on sessions trained, not on how well they went
Existing eligibility rules are unchanged: asleep blocks, sick blocks, insufficient energy blocks, all with their current messages
Every existing assertion in Tests/TrainingTests.swift about cost and blocking still passes
Grading is pure and unit-tested at every boundary
Add new files to project.yml and re-run xcodegen generate
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Training minigame - Timing Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stop a moving marker in a target zone, so training takes skill.
acceptanceCriteria: A marker sweeps a bar, tapping stops it, and the zone hit decides the grade
A centre sub-zone yields perfect; missing the zone entirely yields miss
Sweep speed and zone width are injectable so tests never wait real time
Grade-from-position is pure and unit-tested at each zone boundary
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Training minigame - Button Masher
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap as fast as I can against a timer.
acceptanceCriteria: Counts taps within a fixed window and maps the count to a grade by threshold
The window duration is injectable
A live tap count is visible during play
Grade-from-count is pure and unit-tested at each threshold
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Training minigame - Power Meter
userStories.description: As a user, I want to hold to charge and release before it overloads.
acceptanceCriteria: Holding fills a meter and releasing within the target band grades highest
Overfilling past the band yields miss, so there is a real cost to greed
Fill rate and band bounds are injectable
Grade-from-fill is pure and unit-tested including the overload case

userStories.title: Training minigame - Crown Sprint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to spin the Digital Crown to fill a gauge.
acceptanceCriteria: Crown rotation accumulates toward a target within a time window
Uses digitalCrownRotation and the gauge responds to real crown input in the Simulator
Rotation target and window are injectable
Grade-from-rotation is pure and unit-tested
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes

userStories.title: Training minigame - Reflex Strike
userStories.description: As a user, I want to tap the instant the signal appears.
acceptanceCriteria: After a randomised delay a signal appears and reaction time decides the grade
Tapping BEFORE the signal is a false start and yields miss
The delay uses the project's existing SeededGenerator so a test can pin it
Grade-from-latency is pure and unit-tested including the false start
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes

userStories.title: Training minigame - Sequence Recall
userStories.description: As a user, I want to repeat a pattern back from memory.
acceptanceCriteria: A short sequence is shown then reproduced by the user, and the correct count decides the grade
A fully correct sequence yields perfect; the first wrong entry ends the round
Sequence length and playback speed are injectable, and generation uses SeededGenerator
Grade-from-correct-count is pure and unit-tested
Verify in the watchOS Simulator via a #if DEBUG launch flag, and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Assign a minigame per Digimon
userStories.description: As a player, I want a new Digimon to bring a new training game, so raising one is worth it.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the six shipped lines maps to a DIFFERENT one of the six games
A Digimon outside the six lines falls back deterministically by stage - never to 'no game'
The same Digimon ALWAYS gets the same game; assignment is not random per session
Assignment is pure and unit-tested including the fallback
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)

userStories.title: Train opens the assigned minigame
userStories.description: As a user, I want tapping Train to start my Digimon's game rather than silently adding a point.
acceptanceCriteria: Train presents the assigned game as an overlay, in the manner of BattleView and EvolutionCeremonyView - the buttons underneath are not tappable through it
The result applies the graded gain and shows what was earned
Blocked cases (asleep, sick, insufficient energy) show their message and NEVER open a game
Dismissing or backgrounding mid-game grades as miss and does not refund, so it is not an escape hatch from a bad round
Verify in the watchOS Simulator end to end - open, play, see the gain - and record the screenshot result in notes
Typecheck passes (xcodebuild build succeeds)
Tests pass
